reference: My son is twenty-four years old and very active; he is not overweight. He has had flat feet all his life. It became really noticeable when he played football. The pants made it very obvious that his knees kind of turned in. He went to a doctor and got inserts for his shoes, which seemed to help somewhat, but now he is suffering from constant back pain. I am just trying to figure out what kind of doctor I should take him to: an orthopedic doctor, a back doctor, or a foot doctor?
text: My son is 24 years old and very active. He is not overweight. He has had flat feet all his life. It became really noticeable when he played football. The pants made it very obvious that his knees kind of turned in. He went to a doctor and got inserts for his shoes, which seemed to help somewhat. But now he is suffering from constant back pain. I am just trying to figure out what kind of doctor I should take him to: an orthopedic doctor, a back doctor, or a foot doctor.
